goal: Write failing test shells from specs
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Skill file exists at skills/invariants-audit/SKILL.md following size budget (<=120 prose lines, sibling refs, stop conditions footer)
AC1a: init.sh and upgrade.sh install to .claude/skills/invariants-audit/; byte-identity sync test verifies source/installed parity
AC2: Skill teaches 5 review categories — state-machine bugs, blocked/rejected/retry paths, spec-impl-test-hook contradictions, fixture-template-validator mismatches, syntax-not-behavior tests
AC3: Skill guides 5-step invariant analysis — identify invariant, where encoded, where enforced, where tested, failure paths
AC4: Claude commands reference the skill — review.md, architect.md, security-gate.md, test-design.md each have Skill References table entry
AC5: Codex reviewer prompts contain ## Invariant Checks section with Apply when trigger and checklist — review-code.md, review-prd.md, review-architecture.md, review-test-design.md
AC6: Wiring contract tests verify Claude command rows, Codex reviewer sections, and structural anchors without phrase-binding
AC7: Skill documents when to use invariants-audit vs normal review with clear trigger conditions
